title: The day Marine Le Pen took back control of her fate, despite a conviction
subhead: After being sentenced to one year of house arrest, the French far right's leader found an opening back into the presidential race.

title: Le Pen cuts short first presidential campaign trip amid protests
US carries out another round of strikes on Iran after Trump says ceasefire is over
Graham Platner, Democratic candidate for US Senate, exits race after rape allegation
subhead: Platner, the Democratic Senate nominee for Maine and the party's hope to flip a Republican-held seat, ended his campaign following a rape allegation. He has denied the accusations.

title: EWC 2026 in Paris: Behind the scenes of the controversial partnership between France and Saudi Arabia in esports
subhead: By hosting this Saudi video game tournament in Paris, with an opening ceremony on Wednesday, July 8, featuring a concert by Aya Nakamura, the Elysée is endorsing the kingdom's dominance in the sector – but not without facing criticism from the French esports community.

title: Tour de France: Kooij wins 5th stage in chaotic sprint finish
subhead: Olav Kooij, a 24-year-old Dutch debutant Tour contender, beat German Max Kanter into second, with Belgian Tim Merlier coming in third place for the Tour de France's fifth stage, after several riders crashed and others were slowed.

title: 2026 World Cup: Recent FIFA decisions ramp up pressure on referees
subhead: From the Balogun case to the appointment of an all-Argentinian refereeing team for the France-Morocco match on Thursday, FIFA itself has shaken the refereeing corps, undermining their authority and fueling suspicions of bias.

Marine Le Pen, phoenix of French far right, embarks on Elysée race
subhead: Marine Le Pen announced she would run for president hours after her embezzlement conviction was upheld.

title: Pyrénées wildfires destroy everything in their path: 'I didn't think I'd make it out alive'
subhead: The residents of Ille-sur-Têt, a town that was evacuated, remain in shock as the Trévillach wildfire – the largest the region has seen in 50 years – was still not under control on Monday evening.

title: Albert Camus archives, including manuscripts, forged documents and letters, join national collection
subhead: The archives, previously kept within the writer's family, has been acquired by France's national library for €9 million with support from Hermès and CIC.

Climate change: 'We cannot meet the challenge of adapting housing with the tools of the past. A thorough overhaul is needed'
